design_summary: A small SRAM scratchpad controller with explicit OpenRAM-style memory intent, a firmware-visible MMIO register map, and MBIST-facing status/control hooks suitable for downstream memory integration and MBIST collateral generation.

fixed_assumptions: reset_n is an active-low synchronous reset sampled on clk.
SRAM depth is fixed at 64 words and data width is fixed at 32 bits.
The controller uses a memory-mapped register interface internally; firmware access is represented by the top-level control/data ports specified in the contract.

If an OpenRAM macro collateral artifact is available, the SRAM macro name to instantiate is demo_sram_32x64; otherwise a synthesizable fallback wrapper named demo_sram_32x64_model must be used with the same port contract.
The memory path must remain recognizable as SRAM-like for synthesis and DFT/MBIST tools.

top_module.name: sram_mbist_demo_controller
top_module.description: Top-level controller that exposes firmware-visible SRAM access controls, BIST request/status, IRQ generation, and a direct single-port SRAM interface to the OpenRAM-compatible 64x32 memory.
top_module.functionality: Implements a simple scratchpad controller around a 64x32 single-port SRAM. The controller accepts explicit write and read controls, maintains software-visible status outputs, and provides an MBIST request path through bist_start. The controller must preserve memory-like structure visibility so that downstream synthesis and DFT flows can detect the SRAM macro or fallback model, infer MBIST hooks, and generate collateral. The top-level outputs ready, bist_done, bist_fail, and irq are driven from internal status state. The read data output rd_data reflects the last captured SRAM read data or the software-visible readback value as defined by the internal memory access sequencing.
responsibilities: Gate and sequence single-port SRAM accesses from write/read control inputs.
Expose a recognizable SRAM macro interface for OpenRAM collateral or synthesizable fallback memory.
Latch and present BIST completion and failure state.
Generate irq when IRQ enable is set and a BIST result is latched.
Provide explicit reset semantics for control, status, IRQ, and read data.
Keep the design synthesizable and free of vendor-only primitives.
top_module.reset_behavior: On synchronous assertion of reset_n low, clear ready deassertion state, clear bist_done, clear bist_fail, clear irq, and clear software-visible rd_data to 0. Control register state and BIST status state are reset to their default disabled/idle values. The SRAM contents are not architecturally required to be cleared by reset unless implemented by the chosen memory model.
behavior_rules: The top-level memory interface must remain single-port and SRAM-like.

Write accesses have priority over read accesses only if both are asserted simultaneously on the same cycle; otherwise the implementation may define a deterministic arbitration policy but must document it in RTL comments and testbench expectations.

The memory address space is 8-bit at the top-level interface but only the lower 6 bits are used to address the 64-word SRAM; upper bits are ignored or may be reserved for future decode without affecting SRAM access.
bist_start shall initiate the BIST request path and set a running condition until completion or failure is reported.
IRQ must assert only when IRQ_ENABLE is set in the control register and a BIST done/fail condition is latched in status.
The design must provide a clear separation between transport signals and semantic status signals for MBIST collateral generation.
The top-level port set is fixed and no additional external bus interface is implied.

modules.name: sram_mbist_demo_controller_regs
modules.description: Internal MMIO/register and control sequencer block that decodes firmware-style accesses, generates explicit semantic control signals, tracks status, and routes SRAM read/write operations and BIST control.
modules.functionality: Implements the controller-visible register contract and emits explicit semantic signals for memory access control, BIST request, IRQ enable, and status tracking. It decodes the register map fields CONTROL, STATUS, MEM_ADDR, MEM_WDATA, MEM_RDATA, MEM_CONTROL, BIST_CONTROL, BIST_STATUS, IRQ_STATUS, and IRQ_CLEAR. The module must produce concrete outputs for memory command and status semantics rather than burying them in implicit transport buses. It also owns the software-visible read data presentation and the status latch behavior for bist_done and bist_fail.
responsibilities: Decode CONTROL, STATUS, MEM_ADDR, MEM_WDATA, MEM_RDATA, MEM_CONTROL, BIST_CONTROL, BIST_STATUS, IRQ_STATUS, and IRQ_CLEAR semantics.
Generate explicit SRAM control outputs csb, web, addr, din semantics for the top-level controller.
Generate explicit BIST request and clear-result pulses.
Track ready, BIST running, done, fail, IRQ enable, and last failure address semantics.
Provide the software-visible read data path and readback muxing for register space.
Ensure clear and reset actions restore all status to defaults.
modules.reset_behavior: Synchronously clears all control registers, status latches, readback output, IRQ state, memory request outputs, BIST request outputs, and last failure address. ready is driven to its reset/idle value. bist_done and bist_fail are cleared. irq is deasserted. rd_data is cleared to 32'h00000000.
behavior_rules: CONTROL bit 0 shall represent ENABLE, bit 1 SOFT_RESET, bit 2 IRQ_ENABLE.
STATUS shall present ready, bist_done, bist_fail, and busy semantics.
MEM_ADDR shall capture a 6-bit SRAM address view.
MEM_WDATA shall capture 32-bit SRAM write data.
MEM_RDATA shall present 32-bit read-only data sourced from SRAM readback or the current read pipeline value.
MEM_CONTROL bit 0 shall request a write and bit 1 shall request a read.
BIST_CONTROL bit 0 shall request start and bit 1 shall request clear-result.
BIST_STATUS shall expose done, fail, running, and last_fail_addr[5:0].
IRQ_STATUS shall expose bist_done and bist_fail latched status.
IRQ_CLEAR shall clear IRQ_STATUS bits when written with one-hot or bitwise ones on the corresponding clear fields, as implemented consistently in RTL.
irq shall assert only when irq_enable and one of the latched IRQ_STATUS bits is set.
Semantic control outputs must be explicit ports and must not be inferred implicitly from register transport data.
Memory address top bits are ignored for SRAM addressing but may be used for future software decode if desired without changing SRAM behavior.

modules.name: demo_sram_32x64_model
modules.description: Synthesizable single-port SRAM fallback wrapper with the same memory interface expected by OpenRAM collateral, preserving memory-like structure for integration and MBIST collateral generation.
modules.functionality: Implements a synthesizable, vendor-neutral, single-port SRAM model with the exact interface expected by the OpenRAM-compatible macro. The wrapper must infer as memory-like structure in synthesis and must not use vendor-specific primitives. It should support read and write behavior controlled by csb and web, with dout updating according to a single-port SRAM style synchronous access model suitable for simulation and synthesis fallback.
responsibilities: Provide a synthesizable fallback when OpenRAM collateral is unavailable.
Maintain the same interface contract as the OpenRAM-compatible macro.
Preserve memory-like inference for synthesis and DFT flows.
Support deterministic simulation behavior for reads and writes.
modules.reset_behavior: On reset, the output data may be cleared to a known value for simulation determinism; the stored memory contents may be left unchanged unless the implementation explicitly models memory reset. The wrapper must remain synthesizable and must not require memory initialization for correct elaboration.
behavior_rules: Use a single-port read/write memory contract with active-low chip select and active-low write enable semantics as mapped by the controller.
Do not instantiate vendor-only memories or black-box constructs in the fallback model.
The interface name and width must match the OpenRAM-compatible macro contract exactly.
The memory depth is 64 words of 32 bits and the address width is 6 bits.
The wrapper should be suitable for replacement by an OpenRAM hard macro without changing the top-level controller contract.

top_level_connections.description: Single synchronous clock distributed to the register/control block and the SRAM model or macro wrapper.
Synchronous active-low reset is consumed by the register/control block.
Firmware write strobe into the internal register/control block.
Firmware write address into the internal register/control block.
Firmware write data into the internal register/control block.
Firmware read strobe into the internal register/control block.
Firmware read address into the internal register/control block.
External BIST request input into the internal control block.
Top-level read data output driven by the internal register/control block.
Top-level ready output driven by the internal register/control block.
Top-level BIST done output driven by the internal register/control block.
Top-level BIST fail output driven by the internal register/control block.
Top-level interrupt output driven by the internal register/control block.

inter_module_signals.name: sram_dout
inter_module_signals.description: Raw single-port SRAM read data returning from the memory model into the register/control block.

inter_module_signals.name: sram_csb
inter_module_signals.description: Active-low SRAM chip select generated by the register/control block.

inter_module_signals.name: sram_web
inter_module_signals.description: Active-low SRAM write enable generated by the register/control block.

inter_module_signals.name: sram_addr
inter_module_signals.description: Six-bit SRAM word address generated by the register/control block.

inter_module_signals.name: sram_din
inter_module_signals.description: Write data driven into the SRAM by the register/control block.

fields.name: SOFT_RESET
fields.description: Software reset request; self-clearing or synchronous pulse as implemented.

fields.name: IRQ_ENABLE
fields.description: Interrupt enable for BIST result events.

fields.name: busy
fields.description: Busy indicator, typically reflecting BIST running.

fields.name: last_fail_addr
fields.description: Last failing address[5:0] packed into the readback field.

fields.name: bist_done
fields.description: Latched interrupt status for BIST done.

fields.name: bist_fail
fields.description: Latched interrupt status for BIST fail.

fields.name: clear_bist_done
fields.description: Write one to clear bist_done interrupt status.

fields.name: clear_bist_fail
fields.description: Write one to clear bist_fail interrupt status.
